practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
practicals.details: Lab activities from prescribed books including constructing square root spiral and representing rational/irrational numbers.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab (Euclid's Axioms)
practicals.details: Construct bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints.

practicals.title: Circumcircle Construction Lab
practicals.details: Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle using geometric tools.

practicals.title: Coordinate Geometry Distance Calculation Lab
practicals.details: Compute the distance between two points and determine if three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.

practicals.title: Area Calculation Lab (Heron's Formula)
practicals.details: Compute the area of triangles from sides using Heron's formula.

practicals.title: 3-D Shape Modelling Lab
practicals.details: Visualise 2D representations of 3D objects to solve problems involving surface area and volume.

practicals.title: Probability Simulation Lab
practicals.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation to model random experiments and estimate probabilities.

activities.title: Constructing a Square with Baudhayana's Sulbasutras Method
activities.details: Students construct a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras to understand ancient geometric constructions.

activities.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
activities.unit_name: UNIT I: NUMBER SYSTEM (Inferred from context)
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle Solving
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to visualize Geometric Progressions and apply computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities with Models
activities.details: Use geometric models, algebra tiles (with and without), or paper manipulation to visualise factorisation of quadratic expressions.

activities.title: Floor Plan Grid Representation
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Paper Manipulation
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis (Dharmachakra/Ashoka Chakra)
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra and Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Real-world Circle Applications Study
activities.details: Investigate how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Empirical Probability Experiments
activities.details: Perform experiments to estimate the empirical probability of events and represent them using tree diagrams.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi Research
projects.details: Research historical approximations to π (Archimedes, Aryabhata, Zu Chongzhi) and explain ancient methods for circle area.

projects.title: Squaring a Rectangle Investigation
projects.details: Investigate the proof of 'squaring a rectangle' from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

projects.title: Brahmagupta's Formula Derivation Project
projects.details: Derive Brahmagupta's formula for the area of a cyclic 4-gon and explain its relationship to Heron's formula.

projects.title: 3-D Shape Surface Area and Volume Analysis
projects.details: Systematically calculate and compare surface areas and volumes of 3-D shapes by varying dimensions.

projects.title: Weighted Average Application Study
projects.details: Apply weighted average concepts in different real-life settings and analyze statistical data.

marks_distribution: Unit VI: Statistics and Probability - 10 Marks

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with emphasis on logical thinking, problem-solving, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
Specific competencies mapped: C-1.1 (Number System), C-3.2/C-9 (Algebra), C-4.5/CG-9 (Coordinate Geometry), C-7.1/C-4.2 (Geometry), C-5.1/CG-9 (Mensuration), C-6.1/C-6.2 (Statistics & Probability).
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (5+5): 10 Marks

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

project_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects.

project_assessment.context: Integral part of the learning process moving beyond summative examinations.

practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
practicals.details: Lab activities from prescribed books including constructing square root spiral and representing rational/irrational numbers.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab (Euclid's Axioms)
practicals.details: Construct bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints.

practicals.title: Circumcircle Construction Lab
practicals.details: Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle using geometric tools.

practicals.title: Coordinate Geometry Distance Calculation Lab
practicals.details: Compute the distance between two points and determine if three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.

practicals.title: Area Calculation Lab (Heron's Formula)
practicals.details: Compute the area of triangles from sides using Heron's formula.

practicals.title: 3-D Shape Modelling Lab
practicals.details: Visualise 2D representations of 3D objects to solve problems involving surface area and volume.

practicals.title: Probability Simulation Lab
practicals.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation to model random experiments and estimate probabilities.

activities.title: Constructing a Square with Baudhayana's Sulbasutras Method
activities.details: Students construct a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras to understand ancient geometric constructions.

activities.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
activities.unit_name: UNIT I: NUMBER SYSTEM (Inferred from context)
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle Solving
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to visualize Geometric Progressions and apply computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities with Models
activities.details: Use geometric models, algebra tiles (with and without), or paper manipulation to visualise factorisation of quadratic expressions.

activities.title: Floor Plan Grid Representation
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Paper Manipulation
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis (Dharmachakra/Ashoka Chakra)
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra and Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Real-world Circle Applications Study
activities.details: Investigate how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Empirical Probability Experiments
activities.details: Perform experiments to estimate the empirical probability of events and represent them using tree diagrams.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi Research
projects.details: Research historical approximations to π (Archimedes, Aryabhata, Zu Chongzhi) and explain ancient methods for circle area.

projects.title: Squaring a Rectangle Investigation
projects.details: Investigate the proof of 'squaring a rectangle' from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

projects.title: Brahmagupta's Formula Derivation Project
projects.details: Derive Brahmagupta's formula for the area of a cyclic 4-gon and explain its relationship to Heron's formula.

projects.title: 3-D Shape Surface Area and Volume Analysis
projects.details: Systematically calculate and compare surface areas and volumes of 3-D shapes by varying dimensions.

projects.title: Weighted Average Application Study
projects.details: Apply weighted average concepts in different real-life settings and analyze statistical data.

marks_distribution: Unit VI: Statistics and Probability - 10 Marks

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with emphasis on logical thinking, problem-solving, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
Specific competencies mapped: C-1.1 (Number System), C-3.2/C-9 (Algebra), C-4.5/CG-9 (Coordinate Geometry), C-7.1/C-4.2 (Geometry), C-5.1/CG-9 (Mensuration), C-6.1/C-6.2 (Statistics & Probability).
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (5+5): 10 Marks

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

project_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects.

project_assessment.context: Integral part of the learning process moving beyond summative examinations.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with emphasis on logical thinking, problem-solving, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
Specific competencies mapped: C-1.1 (Number System), C-3.2/C-9 (Algebra), C-4.5/CG-9 (Coordinate Geometry), C-7.1/C-4.2 (Geometry), C-5.1/CG-9 (Mensuration), C-6.1/C-6.2 (Statistics & Probability).
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (5+5): 10 Marks

project_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects.

project_assessment.context: Integral part of the learning process moving beyond summative examinations.

competency_groups.title: Understands numbers and number sets
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: C-1.1: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.

competency_groups.title: Builds deductive and inductive logic for number proofs
competency_groups.description: Proves theorems related to numbers, powers, exponents, recursion relations, and sums of square numbers.
competencies: C-2.1: Understanding of powers (radical powers) and exponents.

competency_groups.title: Discovers algebraic identities and models real-life situations with equations
competency_groups.description: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm; solves contextualized problems using linear or polynomial equations.
competencies: C-3.1: States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, and division algorithm.

C-3.2: Models and solves contextualised problems using equations (simultaneous linear equations in two variables or single polynomial equations), and draws conclusions about a situation being modelled.
C-3.3: Learns Brahmagupta's quadratic formula (in both symbolic and poetic form) and its derivation, and uses it to solve some of the poetic puzzles of Bhaskara as well as modern-day problems.

competency_groups.title: Analyses characteristics of 2D geometric shapes and proves relationships
competency_groups.description: Describes congruence, proves theorems using Euclid's axioms for triangles/quadrilaterals/circles; specifies locations via coordinate geometry.
competencies: C-4.1: Describes relationships including congruence of two-dimensional geometric shapes (such as lines, angles, triangles) to make and test conjectures and solve problems.
C-4.2: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for triangles and quadrilaterals, and applies them to solve geometric problems.
C-4.3: Proves theorems about the geometry of a circle, including its chords, subtended angles, inscribed polygons, and area in terms of pi.

C-4.5: Specifies locations and describes spatial relationships using coordinate geometry, for example, plotting a pair of linear equations and graphically finding the solution, or finding the area of triangle with given coordinates as vertices.

competency_groups.title: Derives and uses formulae for areas and volumes
competency_groups.description: Visualises, represents, and calculates area of triangles using Heron's/Brahmagupta's formulas; discovers formulae for surface areas and volumes of solid objects.
competencies: C-5.1: Visualises, represents, and calculates the area of a triangle using Heron's formula and its generalisation to cyclic quadrilaterals given by Brahmagupta's formula.

C-5.2: Visualises and uses mathematical thinking to discover formulae to calculate surface areas and volumes of solid objects (cubes, cuboids, spheres, hemispheres, right circular cylinders or cones, and their combinations).

competency_groups.title: Analyses data using statistical concepts and probability
competency_groups.description: Applies measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and concepts from probability to solve problems on likelihood.
competencies: C-6.1: Applies measures of central tendencies, such as mean, median, and mode.
C-6.2: Applies concepts from probability to solve problems on the likelihood of everyday events.

competency_groups.title: Perceives and appreciates axiomatic structure of Mathematics
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements, carries out constructions using assumptions/axioms/postulates; visualises proofs for algebraic identities.
competencies: C-7.1: Proves mathematical statements and carries out geometric constructions using stated assumptions, axioms, postulates, definitions, and mathematics vocabulary.
C-7.2: Visualises and appreciates geometric proofs for algebraic identities and other 'proofs without words'.
C-7.3: Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms and postulates for angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, circles, area-related theorems for triangles, and parallelograms.
C-7.4: Constructs different geometrical shapes like bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons, satisfying given constraints.

competency_groups.title: Builds skills in visualisation, optimisation, representation, and modelling
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena using graphs/tables/equations; uses 2D representations of 3D objects for problem-solving.
competencies: C-8.1: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions.
C-8.2: Uses two-dimensional representations of three-dimensional objects to visualise and solve problems, such as those involving surface area and volume.
C-8.3: Employs optimisation strategies to maximise desired quantities (such as area, volume, or other output) under given constraints.

competency_groups.title: Develops computational thinking for complex problems
competency_groups.description: Breaks down complex problems into simple sub-problems; describes sequences of instructions; analyses similarities/differences to create general solutions.
competencies: C-9.1: Decomposes a problem into sub-problems.
C-9.2: Describes and analyses a sequence of instructions being followed.
C-9.3: Analyses similarities and differences among problems to make one solution or procedure work for multiple problems.
C-9.4: Engages in algorithmic problem-solving to design such solutions.

competency_groups.title: Knows and appreciates contributions of mathematicians from India and around the world
competency_groups.description: Recognises important historical and modern contributions to Mathematics, including evolution of numbers, geometry, algebra.
competencies: C-10.1: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics (such as the evolution of numbers, geometry, and algebra).
C-10.2: Recognises modern contributions to Mathematics made in both India and abroad, and understands the next frontiers and next major open questions in the field of Mathematics.

competency_groups.title: Explores connections of Mathematics with other subjects
competency_groups.description: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems in Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.
competencies: C-11.1: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations in multiple subjects across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.

practicals.title: Mathematics Laboratory Activities (Prescribed Books)
practicals.details: Lab activities from prescribed books including constructing square root spiral and representing rational/irrational numbers.

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions Lab (Euclid's Axioms)
practicals.details: Construct bisectors of line segments, angles and their bisectors, triangles, and other polygons satisfying given constraints.

practicals.title: Circumcircle Construction Lab
practicals.details: Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle using geometric tools.

practicals.title: Coordinate Geometry Distance Calculation Lab
practicals.details: Compute the distance between two points and determine if three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.

practicals.title: Area Calculation Lab (Heron's Formula)
practicals.details: Compute the area of triangles from sides using Heron's formula.

practicals.title: 3-D Shape Modelling Lab
practicals.details: Visualise 2D representations of 3D objects to solve problems involving surface area and volume.

practicals.title: Probability Simulation Lab
practicals.details: Use computational thinking strategies like simulation to model random experiments and estimate probabilities.

activities.title: Constructing a Square with Baudhayana's Sulbasutras Method
activities.details: Students construct a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras to understand ancient geometric constructions.

activities.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
activities.unit_name: UNIT I: NUMBER SYSTEM (Inferred from context)
activities.details: Construct the square root spiral to represent rational and irrational numbers through visual models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle Solving
activities.details: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to visualize Geometric Progressions and apply computational thinking.

activities.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities with Models
activities.details: Use geometric models, algebra tiles (with and without), or paper manipulation to visualise factorisation of quadratic expressions.

activities.title: Floor Plan Grid Representation
activities.details: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Geometric Constructions with Paper Manipulation
activities.details: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Analysis (Dharmachakra/Ashoka Chakra)
activities.details: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, such as the Dharmachakra and Ashoka Chakra.

activities.title: Real-world Circle Applications Study
activities.details: Investigate how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.

activities.title: Data Collection and Graphical Representation
activities.details: Collect, organise, visualise data to answer a statistical investigative question using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Empirical Probability Experiments
activities.details: Perform experiments to estimate the empirical probability of events and represent them using tree diagrams.

projects.title: Historical Approximations of Pi Research
projects.details: Research historical approximations to π (Archimedes, Aryabhata, Zu Chongzhi) and explain ancient methods for circle area.

projects.title: Squaring a Rectangle Investigation
projects.details: Investigate the proof of 'squaring a rectangle' from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras.

projects.title: Brahmagupta's Formula Derivation Project
projects.details: Derive Brahmagupta's formula for the area of a cyclic 4-gon and explain its relationship to Heron's formula.

projects.title: 3-D Shape Surface Area and Volume Analysis
projects.details: Systematically calculate and compare surface areas and volumes of 3-D shapes by varying dimensions.

projects.title: Weighted Average Application Study
projects.details: Apply weighted average concepts in different real-life settings and analyze statistical data.

marks_distribution: Unit VI: Statistics and Probability - 10 Marks

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.
Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria.
Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units (CG-1 to CG-11) with emphasis on logical thinking, problem-solving, mathematical modelling, and computational thinking.
Specific competencies mapped: C-1.1 (Number System), C-3.2/C-9 (Algebra), C-4.5/CG-9 (Coordinate Geometry), C-7.1/C-4.2 (Geometry), C-5.1/CG-9 (Mensuration), C-6.1/C-6.2 (Statistics & Probability).
internal_assessment: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (5+5): 10 Marks

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books.
prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)

project_assessment.description: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects.

project_assessment.context: Integral part of the learning process moving beyond summative examinations.
